SWKS.title: Skyworks Solutions (SKWS) Stock Is Trending Overnight: Here's Why

macro_events.event_summary: Lufthansa warns of nearly $2 billion in additional fuel costs due to Middle East conflict forcing route diversions and elevated jet fuel prices.
primary_drivers: Elevated jet fuel/crude prices from Middle East supply risk
Airspace closures forcing longer reroutes (more fuel burn, lower aircraft utilization)
Margin compression for fuel-exposed, long-haul carriers

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; benefits directly from sustained higher crude/refined product prices driving the fuel cost spike Lufthansa is citing.
Same upstream crude leverage as XOM; Middle East conflict premium in oil supports realized prices and refining crack spreads on jet fuel.
Pure-play refiner with strong distillate/jet fuel exposure; jet fuel crack spreads widen when geopolitical supply fears hit while demand is intact.
Domestic-only US carrier with no transatlantic/Middle East routing exposure and historically heaviest fuel hedging program among US airlines — relative outperformer vs. legacy carriers on this story.

losers.rationale: Largest US transatlantic carrier with significant European/Middle East/India routing; same reroute and fuel cost dynamic Lufthansa flagged hits Delta's international segment directly. Delta also runs its own refinery (Trainer) but net exposure to jet fuel is still negative.
Heaviest US international long-haul exposure (Asia/Europe/India routes) of US3; most reliant on routes that get rerouted around Middle East airspace, mirroring Lufthansa's cost structure.
Unhedged fuel program and weakest balance sheet among legacy US carriers — most operating-leverage downside to a sustained jet fuel spike.
Thin margins, no fuel hedging, transatlantic expansion exposure; high sensitivity to jet fuel moves on a low-margin base.

headlines: Lufthansa faces nearly $2 billion in extra fuel costs amid Middle East conflict
